metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board and Timers
- 1 main game board with 3 action rows — each row has multiple action spaces with 2 columns per space
- 3 sand timers of different durations:
  - **Black timer** (45 seconds) — fast-cycling, small rewards
  - **Green timer** (2 minutes) — moderate actions
  - **Purple timer** (3 minutes) — slowest, most powerful actions
- Each action space has a "before timer" side (left) and an "after timer" side (right), separated by a timer slot

#### Player Components (per player)
- 1 asymmetric character mat (double-sided: basic + advanced) with unique abilities and starting resources
- 2 worker pawns: 1 regular worker + 1 grande worker (can share spaces with other players' workers)
- 4 track markers for Power, Prestige, Popularity, and Legendary Achievement
- 1 VP marker

#### Cards
- **Province cards** — engine-building cards providing ongoing bonuses, resource production, or end-game scoring. Tucked under player board by action color.
- **Stratagem cards** — 4 unique cards per character side (40 total across all 10 character sides). One-time or ongoing rule-bending abilities.
- **Council Reward cards** — earned during Council Phase for track advancement.
- **Achievement cards** — bonus end-game scoring conditions.

#### Resources and Tokens
- Gold, vote, military strength, and prestige tokens
- Round marker (tracks rounds 1-4)
- Maximum 10 influence tokens per player

#### Tracks
- 4 advancement tracks: Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement
- Each track has a **threshold line** — you must cross all 4 thresholds into the "parchment" zone to be eligible to win

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Real-Time Simultaneous Play
Pendulum has **NO turns**. All players act simultaneously during the Council Phase. You place workers, flip timers, and collect rewards in real time. The theme: powerful nobles vying to succeed the Timeless King as ruler of Dunya.

#### The Timer Mechanism
3 sand timers sit on the board, one per action row. Each action space has two columns:
- **"Before" column (left)**: Place your worker here WHILE the timer is NOT on this row.
- **"After" column (right)**: Workers move here when the timer arrives and is flipped, allowing you to collect the action's reward.

#### The Action Cycle
1. **Place a worker** on the "before" side of an action space on a row where the timer is NOT present.
2. **Wait** for the timer to be flipped onto that row.
3. **Any player may flip** a timer once its sand runs out — this is voluntary, not automatic. Delaying a flip is a strategic choice.
4. When flipped, ALL workers on the "before" side of that row slide to the "after" side.
5. **Retrieve your worker** from the "after" side to gain the action's reward.
6. Worker is now free to be placed again.

#### Round Structure (4 rounds)
Each round has two phases:
1. **Council Phase** — Real-time simultaneous play. Lasts until the purple timer has been flipped **3 times** (~9 minutes of real time). All players place/retrieve workers, flip timers, gain resources, and advance tracks.
2. **Rest Phase** — Sequential cleanup:
   1. Determine Privilege based on vote tokens.
   2. Gain Council Reward cards in Privilege order (1st place: first pick + 2 VP advances; 2nd/3rd: 1 VP advance).
   3. Discard down to 2 province cards per color.
   4. Reset board with new cards and tokens.
   5. Optionally reposition workers.

#### Key Constraints
- Regular workers: 1 per space (exception: black section has unlimited capacity).
- Grande worker: may share a space with other players' workers.
- You CANNOT place on a row where the timer has run out and is waiting to be flipped.
- You CANNOT retrieve from the "before" side — workers must wait for the timer.
- Workers on "after" block you from placing on the "before" side of the same space until retrieved.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Timer Flipping Rules
- Any player may flip a timer once the sand runs out — you don't need workers on that row.
- When flipped, ALL workers on the "before" side simultaneously move to the "after" side.
- **Flipping is voluntary.** No rule forces anyone to flip. Delaying is a strategic tool — block opponents from retrieving, or buy time to place your own worker first.
- Black timer (45 seconds) cycles fastest, giving frequent small rewards. Purple timer (3 minutes) cycles slowest, gating the most powerful actions behind longer waits.
- A round ends when the purple timer has been flipped 3 times.

#### Worker Placement Rules
- **Regular worker**: Only on unoccupied "before" spaces (exception: black section has unlimited capacity — any number of workers can share).
- **Grande worker**: May be placed regardless of other workers present. However, a regular worker CANNOT be placed on a space that already has a grande worker (unless in the black section).
- You must retrieve a worker from "after" before placing that same worker again.

#### Action Spaces
Each row contains multiple spaces. Actions include:
- **Gain resources**: Gold, votes, military strength, prestige tokens.
- **Advance a track**: Pay required resources to move up Power, Prestige, or Popularity.
- **Acquire province cards**: Draft from a shared display into your tableau. Tucked under your board by color — future activations of that color grant province card bonuses.
- **Play stratagem cards**: Free action during Council Phase (no worker needed). Powerful rule-bending effects.
- **Convert resources**: Trade one type for another.

Black row = quick, small rewards. Green row = moderate. Purple row = most powerful, longest wait.

#### Province Cards (Engine Building)
- Acquired from shared display via action spaces.
- Tucked under player board organized by action color.
- When you take that action, all province cards in that slot grant bonuses.
- During Council Phase cleanup: discard down to 2 per color.
- Province cards compound over 4 rounds — early acquisition is critical.

#### Stratagem Cards
- 4 unique cards per character side. Played anytime during Council Phase as a free action.
- Effects: extra worker placement, free track advancement, resource windfalls, moving locked workers.
- Some have timing restrictions ("play when you flip a timer," "play during Rest Phase").
- Council Reward cards with lightning bolt icons are one-time use.

#### Character Asymmetry
- 5 double-sided character mats = 10 playable characters. Basic sides are similar; advanced sides diverge significantly.
- Each character has different track lengths for Power, Prestige, and Popularity — threshold distances vary per character.
- Characters include: Bolk ("The Champion"/"The Warmonger"), Licinia ("The Implacable"), Mesoat, Drenkhir, and Gambal ("The Briber").

#### Timeless Mode (Turn-Based Variant)
For players who dislike real-time pressure. Players take sequential turns: place a worker, retrieve a worker, or flip a timer. All other rules remain identical. Recommended for learning.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
After the Rest Phase of Round 4. Exactly 4 rounds — no early termination.

#### Victory Condition: Cross All 4 Thresholds
Each of the 4 tracks (Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement) has a threshold line dividing it into regular and "parchment" zones.

- To be **eligible to win**, ALL 4 markers must be past their thresholds into the parchment zone.
- If only 1 player qualifies: they win.
- If multiple qualify: farthest past thresholds in aggregate wins.
- If no one qualifies: highest aggregate track position wins.

This is the critical strategic tension — you MUST cross all 4 thresholds, not just excel at one.

#### Additional Scoring
- Province card end-game bonuses based on track positions, resources, or conditions.
- Achievement card bonuses.
- VP earned during the game (tracked on VP track).
- Character-specific scoring bonuses.

#### Tiebreakers
1. Compare lowest individual tracker position.
2. Highest Privilege.
3. Most remaining resources.

#### Typical Score Ranges
- First game: Focus on crossing thresholds rather than maximizing.
- Experienced play: All 4 thresholds crossed with province bonuses and achievements stacked.
- The winner is almost always someone who crossed all 4 thresholds — raw VP without threshold eligibility rarely wins.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Learn the Timer Rhythm First
Your first game, just get comfortable with placing, waiting, flipping, and retrieving. Speed comes with practice. The black timer (45 seconds) is your friend — fast cycles, frequent feedback.

#### Balance All 4 Tracks
Because you must cross ALL 4 thresholds to win, ignoring any track is catastrophic. Always prioritize your weakest track. Each character has different track lengths — understand your character's easiest and hardest thresholds.

#### Province Cards Early
Province cards generate bonuses every time you take that action color. Bought in Round 1, a province pays out across 4 rounds of Council Phases. Bought in Round 4, it pays once. Front-load your engine.

#### Don't Ignore the Privilege Track
First pick of Council Rewards + 2 VP advances (vs. 1 for 2nd/3rd place) compounds significantly over 4 Council Phases. Accumulate votes.

#### Grande Worker on Contested Spaces
The grande worker ignores the 1-worker-per-space limit. Place it on high-value spaces where opponents are competing. Avoid parking it on purple (3-min) spaces unless the action is extremely valuable — it'll be locked for a long time.

#### Use Stratagem Cards During Downtime
While waiting for timers to complete, play stratagems for free productivity. Resource generation, worker repositioning, or track advancement at no time cost.

#### Always Have Both Workers Active
While one worker waits for a timer, the other should be placing or collecting. Never let both workers sit idle on "after" spaces.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Timer panic** — Rushing to place without thinking. Slow down mentally even in real-time.
- **Track neglect** — Maxing Power while Prestige sits at zero. Your eligibility is gated by your weakest track.
- **Forgetting to retrieve** — Workers on "after" are locked until retrieved. Every idle moment is wasted.
- **Ignoring the purple row** — 3-minute wait, but most powerful actions. Get a worker there early each round.
- **Late engine building** — Province cards in Rounds 3-4 barely pay off.
- **Not flipping timers** — Flipping is voluntary and strategic. Sometimes flip early to retrieve; sometimes delay to block opponents.

#### Timeless Mode for Learning
If real-time overwhelms, play Timeless Mode first. Same mechanics, no time pressure. Switch to real-time once comfortable.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Pendulum

**Setup**: Take your character mat (basic side for first game). Place track markers at starting positions. Take your 2 workers (1 regular, 1 grande). Place starting resources per your character. Timers go on the board — black (45s), green (2min), purple (3min).

**Round 1, Council Phase Begins**: All timers start running simultaneously. Don't panic.

**Your first move**: Look at the black section (45-second timer). Place your regular worker on a "before" space that gives resources you need. The black timer will run out quickly — in 45 seconds, someone will flip it, and your worker slides to "after." Retrieve it to gain the reward.

**While waiting**: Place your grande worker on a green or purple space. The grande can go anywhere regardless of other workers. Purple spaces are powerful but slow — commit early.

**The rhythm**: Black timer flips every 45 seconds — use it for rapid small gains (resources, conversions). Green timer flips every 2 minutes — moderate rewards. Purple timer flips every 3 minutes — biggest payoff but longest wait. Cycle your regular worker through black spaces while your grande sits on a purple space.

**Track advancement**: Spend resources to advance your Power, Prestige, Popularity, and Legendary Achievement tracks. Check your character mat — which thresholds are closest? Push those first.

**Province cards**: When you can, acquire province cards. Tuck them under your board by color. Every future activation of that color gives you the province bonus too.

**Round ends**: Purple timer flips for the 3rd time. Council Phase ends. Rest Phase: determine Privilege, gain rewards, clean up, reposition workers.

**Rounds 2-4**: Same structure. Engine builds — province cards compound. Push all 4 tracks past their thresholds. Use stratagems for free bonuses.

**Scoring**: Cross all 4 thresholds to be eligible to win. Add province bonuses + achievement bonuses + in-game VP. First game goal: cross at least 3 of 4 thresholds.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Timer Mastery

Pendulum's real-time element means speed matters, but rushing causes mistakes. **Key insight**: you don't have to act during every timer flip. Sometimes waiting for the right timer gives you a better action than frantically placing workers.

#### Worker Specialization

The Grande worker ignores timer restrictions. Use it strategically on high-value actions that would otherwise require waiting. Regular workers should fill consistent, repeatable action slots.

#### Province vs. Council Strategy

**Province cards** give ongoing passive bonuses — prioritize them early. **Council seats** give powerful end-of-round actions. The best players balance both: provinces for the engine, council for the scoring burst.

#### Scoring Track Focus

Three tracks (Military, Prestige, Popularity) but you only need to excel at two. Identify which two tracks synergize with your starting character ability and commit. Spreading thin across all three guarantees mediocrity.

#### Legendary Achievement Timing

Legendary achievements are powerful one-time bonuses. Race to complete them — once claimed by another player, they're gone. Prioritize achievements that align with your chosen tracks.

rulings: Timer flipping is voluntary — no rule forces any player to flip a timer. Strategic delay is legal and intended.
Workers are placed on rows where the timer is NOT present. Actions resolve when the timer returns (is flipped onto) that row.
The black section has unlimited worker capacity — any number of workers from any players can share a space there.
A grande worker CAN be placed on an occupied space. A regular worker CANNOT be placed on a space with a grande worker (except in the black section).
A round ends when the purple (3-minute) timer has been flipped 3 times, lasting approximately 9 minutes of real time.
Province cards gained during an action do NOT trigger during that same action — only on future activations of that color slot.
To be eligible to win, ALL 4 track markers (Power, Prestige, Popularity, Legendary Achievement) must be past their threshold lines into the parchment zone.
Each character has different track lengths — threshold distances vary per character, creating asymmetric difficulty profiles.
